user_message: Can you work with me on forecast prep for Quantum Analytics? Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 13:52 EST
Subject: Re: forecast prep Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 09:19 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Quantum Analytics

There's been a change in our timeline for forecast prep. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Quantum Analytics: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-J trap (No time-sequencing on suggested next steps); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-J failure mode (No time-sequencing on suggested next steps)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
